intitules_isoles: Dina v: Ihaa md Cnio l, Mian
Considered scripture by all Christian churches
Considered scripture by many Christian churches
Was considered for canonization, or is canon in the Ethiopian churches

The serves as list in order of works. It does not serve as a guide to specific editions. The covers used are used for visible aesthetics. You do not have to hunt down every edition used in this graphic. Most texts

listed can be found in The Bible and in the series The Ante-Nicene Fathers, The Nicene and Post-Nicene Fathers published by the Christian Literature Company and T. & T. Clark, and available online at CCEL.org

*The Theologia Germanica is a medieval text by Meister Eckhart. Martin Luther lauded the work and popularized it. This has lead to the misconception that the book was written by Martin Luther, and many
editions in print will make mention of Martin Luther on the cover. It is not a reformation era text added erroneously

**The Philokalia is a somewhat modern collection from 1782, it is not medieval. However, all texts within it were written between the 4th and 15th centuries, and many cannot be found anywhere else but in
The Philokalia. For this reason, it was counted as a medieval work and placed at the end. It seemed essential to include.

She beart of the prudent getteth frowledge; and the ear of the wise seefeth fnowledge.
